name: Chicken Marsala Cordon Bleu
recipeIngredient: 4 chicken breast halves, skinned and boned
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour a little water over both sides of each chicken breast half and place the smooth side down on separate sheets of waxed paper.
2. Fold out the fillet (extra flap) and top with additional waxed paper. Starting at the center, gently pound the breasts until they are between 1/8 and 1/4 inch thick. Remove the top waxed paper and lift the pounded cutlet from the wax paper underneath so it does not stick.
3. In a large skillet over medium heat, melt 2 tablespoons butter and olive oil together. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon flour on a plate, and dredge 2 cutlets to lightly coat. Place in the heated butter and brown well on each side about 2 minutes; set aside covered with aluminum foil.
4. Repeat with remaining 2 cutlets.
5. Add the remaining 1/2 tablespoon butter to the pan with the garlic and rosemary; sauté for 30 seconds, then add the mushrooms; brown well for 1-2 minutes.
6. Pour in the Marsala and add the salt and pepper; boil for about 1 minute. Return all the cutlets in a single layer (slightly overlapping is okay) to the pan.
7. Top each cutlet with a slice of ham and cheese, trimming to fit each breast. Cover the pan and bring to a boil over moderate heat. Lower the heat and simmer until the cheese melts (about 2 minutes).
8. Remove the cutlets from the pan with a spatula and transfer to serving plates, spooning mushroom sauce over top and sprinkling with parsley.
